page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g9it.xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated physical cores with 1 thread per core, utilizing an Intel Xeon 6982P-C processor running at a clock speed of 3.2 GHz. The instance is configured with 16.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced memory-to-core ratio of 4.0 GB per core.

This server type does not include local storage, requiring external block storage volumes for persistent data. It lacks dedicated GPU hardware accelerators and does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 6 Gbps.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated CPU allocation and 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio position this instance as a stable resource for general-purpose workloads. The absence of hyperthreading ensures predictable compute performance per core. It is suitable for application servers, web serving, containerized microservices, and moderate database workloads that do not require local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g9it.xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance built on the x86_64 architecture and managed via the KVM hypervisor. It is equipped with 4 dedicated physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 6982P-C processor operating at 3.2 GHz, with 1 thread per core. The system provides 16.0 GB of RAM, establishing a balanced 4.0 GB per core resource density. This instance does not feature local storage or GPU acceleration, relying on external network storage and baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps. It is designed for workloads requiring dedicated CPU resources without hyperthreading, such as web serving, application hosting, and moderate database operations.

tagline: A dedicated four-core Intel Xeon virtual server with balanced memory and baseline network performance for general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: 4 dedicated physical cores utilizing Intel Xeon 6982P-C processors at 3.2 GHz.
16.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio.
6 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for standard data transfer requirements.
No local storage or GPU hardware included, requiring external storage attachments.
KVM hypervisor-based virtualization with dedicated CPU allocation and single-threaded cores.
Suited for web serving, application hosting, and containerized microservices.
